Rule: Two colours exist. The most frequent one (frame) outlines a closed figure; the other one (block) sits completely inside it. Build a plus from the block without touching the frame. Horizontal arm: look at every row that already contains a block cell, find the nearest frame cell to its left and to its right, take the largest of all left limits and the smallest of all right limits, then paint those rows with the block colour between those two frame cells (exclusive). Vertical arm: for every column that already contains a block cell, find the first frame cell above the block and the first frame cell below it, then paint that entire column with the block colour between those two frame cells (exclusive). Leave everything else unchanged.

Rule: For any enclosed coloured component (one that does not touch the outer frame), paint background cells with that same colour horizontally on every row where the component appears, extending left and right until the first non-background cell is met, and paint background cells vertically on every column where the component appears, extending upward and downward until the first non-background cell is met. The result is the union of the original grid and these horizontal and vertical extensions.
